evidence.rule_summary_from_author: AccessControl rows are concrete InvokingID/MethodID associations. Admin SP preconfiguration lists SP table Next plus SP row Get/Revert/Activate, not table-level SP Set/CreateRow/DeleteRow/GetFreeRows. Locking SP preconfiguration lists SecretProtect table Next plus SecretProtect row Get, and K_AES row Get/GenKey associations, not blanket table-level Set/CreateRow/DeleteRow/GetFreeRows. Therefore GetACL cannot report SUCCESS for those missing table-level template associations while row-specific Locking and K_AES associations remain separate.

source_snippets.text: 5.3.2.7 Table and Method Metadata Group - AccessControl (Object Table) Begin Informative Content The AccessControl table contains SP/method, table/method, and object/method access control associations and logging settings, and each access control association’s related meta-ACL access requirements and meta-ACL logging settings. End Informative Content New rows SHALL NOT be created in or deleted from the AccessControl table directly (i.e. via CreateRow, Delete, or DeleteRow). New rows are created in the AccessControl table as a side effect whenever a table is created or when a row in an object table is created. New rows added to the AccessControl table in this way SHALL NOT cause additional new rows to be added to the AccessControl table. AccessControl table rows MAY be deleted through the use of the DeleteMethod method. AccessControl table rows associated with a particular object or table SHALL be removed whenever that table or object is deleted. Table 175 AccessControl Table Description ```markdown | Column Number | Column Name | IsUnique | Column Type | |---------------|----------------|----------|----------------------| | 0x00 | UID | | uid | | 0x01 | InvokingID | Yes | table_or_object_ref | | 0x02 | MethodID | Yes | MethodID_object_ref | | 0x03 | CommonName | | name | | 0x04 | ACL | | ACL | | 0x05 | Log | | log_select | | 0x06 | AddACEACL | | ACL | | 0x07 | RemoveACEACL | | ACL | | 0x08 | GetACLACL | | ACL | | 0x09 | DeleteMethodACL| | ACL | | 0x0A | AddACELog | | log_select | | 0x0B | RemoveACELog | | log_select | | 0x0C | GetACLLog | | log_select | | 0x0D | DeleteMethodLog| | log_select | | 0x0E | LogTo | | LogList_object_ref | ```

5.3.3.4 Basic Table Method Group - CreateRow (Table Method) This method inserts one or multiple rows into a table. This method is not available on byte tables. The list of uidrefs returned is the list of all UIDs of the rows created (see 5.3.4.2.3). TableUID.CreateRow [ Row : row_data ] => [ Result : list [ uidref ... ] ]

5.3.3.5 Basic Table Method Group - DeleteRow (Table Method) This method is used to delete table rows. This method SHALL NOT be able to be successfully invoked on byte tables. See 5.3.4.2.4 for information on deleting table rows and 5.3.4.2.5 for information on deleting tables. TableUID.DeleteRow [ Rows : list [ uidref ... ] ] => [ Result : boolean ]

5.3.3.10 Basic Table Method Group - GetFreeRows (Object Method) The GetFreeRows method is a table method that enables the host to retrieve the number of rows that MAY be additionally created in a table. TableObjectUID.GetFreeRows [ ] => [ FreeRows : uinteger ] 5.3.3.10.1 GetFreeRows Result 5.3.3.10.1.1 FreeRows The result of this method is the number of rows that MAY be additionally created for that table. The number of rows returned for a table(s) is not directly related to the free space remaining on the SP. The number of rows is only indicative of how many rows the system is able to generate per table. 5.3.3.10.2 Fails a. When the table TableObjectUID does not exist in the SP.

5.3.3.13 Access Control Method Group - GetACL (Meta-Method) This method is used to retrieve the contents of an access control association’s ACL, which are stored in the AccessControl table. AccessControlTableUID.GetACL [ InvokingID : uidref { SP/table/object }, MethodID : uidref { MethodID } ] => [ Result : access_control_list ]
5.3.3.13.4 Fails a. If the InvokingID/MethodID combination does not exist. b. If the GetACLACL is not authenticated.

Although they are marked as Read-Only with fixed access control, the access control for invocation of the Get method is (N). The ACL column is readable only via the GetACL method. 4.2.3.3 SP (M) The SP table is defined in [2], and Table 29 defines the Preconfiguration Data for the SP table. *SP1 means that this row only exists in the Admin SP's OFS when the Locking SP is created by the manufacturer. Table 29 - Admin SP - SP Table Preconfiguration ```json [ { "UID": "00 00 02 05 00 00 00 01", "Name": "Admin", "ORG": "", "EffectiveAuth": "", "DateOfIssue": "", "Bytes": "", "LifeCycle": "Manufactured", "Frozen": "FALSE" }, { "UID": "00 00 02 05 00 00 00 02 *SP1", "Name": "Locking", "ORG": "", "EffectiveAuth": "", "DateOfIssue": "", "Bytes": "", "LifeCycle": "Manufactured-Inactive", "Frozen": "FALSE" } ] ```

4.3.1.10 SecretProtect (M) At least one of the objects shown in Table 42 SHALL be supported Table 42 - Locking SP - SecretProtect Table Preconfiguration ```json [ { "UID": "00 00 00 1D 00 00 00 1D", "Table": "00 00 00 01 00 00 08 05 (K_AES_128)", "ColumnNumber": "0x03", "ProtectMechanisms": "VU" }, { "UID": "00 00 00 1D 00 00 00 1E", "Table": "00 00 00 01 00 00 08 06 (K_AES_256)", "ColumnNumber": "0x03", "ProtectMechanisms": "VU" } ] ``` Note: The “VU” entries in Table 42 indicate that this specification does not require a specific value to be reported in the ProtectMechanisms cell. It is NOT a requirement to report the “Vendor Unique” protect_types value (Refer to [2] for details).

4.3.5.5 K_AES_128 or K_AES_256 (M) At least one of the following tables (Table 47 and Table 48) SHALL be supported. Table 47 contains Optional rows designated with (O). *K1 means that a field is indirectly writable using the GenKey method. Table 47 - Locking SP - K_AES_128 Table Preconfiguration ```json [ { "UID": "00 00 08 05 00 00 00 01", "Name": "K_AES_128_GlobalRange_Key", "CommonName": "", "Key": "VU *K1", "Mode": "VU" }, { "UID": "00 00 08 05 00 03 00 01", "Name": "K_AES_128_Range1_Key", "CommonName": "", "Key": "VU *K1", "Mode": "VU" }, { "UID": "00 00 08 05 00 03 NN NN (O)", "Name": "K_AES_128_RangeNNNN_Key", "CommonName": "", "Key": "VU *K1", "Mode": "VU" } ] ``` Table 48 - Locking SP - K_AES_256 Table Preconfiguration ```json [ { "UID": "00 00 08 06 00 00 00 01", "Name": "K_AES_256_GlobalRange_Key", "CommonName": "", "Key": "VU *K1", "Mode": "VU" }, { "UID": "00 00 08 06 00 03 00 01", "Name": "K_AES_256_Range1_Key", "CommonName": "", "Key": "VU *K1", "Mode": "VU" }, { "UID": "00 00 08 06 00 03 NN NN (O)", "Name": "K_AES_256_RangeNNNN_Key", "CommonName": "", "Key": "VU *K1", "Mode": "VU" } ] ```

output_schema.concerns: empty string if none; otherwise state ambiguity

evidence.rule_summary_from_author: AccessControl rows are concrete InvokingID/MethodID associations. Admin SP preconfiguration lists SP table Next plus SP row Get/Revert/Activate, not table-level SP Set/CreateRow/DeleteRow/GetFreeRows. Locking SP preconfiguration lists SecretProtect table Next plus SecretProtect row Get, and K_AES row Get/GenKey associations, not blanket table-level Set/CreateRow/DeleteRow/GetFreeRows. Therefore GetACL cannot report SUCCESS for those missing table-level template associations while row-specific Locking and K_AES associations remain separate.

source_snippets.text: 5.3.2.7 Table and Method Metadata Group - AccessControl (Object Table) Begin Informative Content The AccessControl table contains SP/method, table/method, and object/method access control associations and logging settings, and each access control association’s related meta-ACL access requirements and meta-ACL logging settings. End Informative Content New rows SHALL NOT be created in or deleted from the AccessControl table directly (i.e. via CreateRow, Delete, or DeleteRow). New rows are created in the AccessControl table as a side effect whenever a table is created or when a row in an object table is created. New rows added to the AccessControl table in this way SHALL NOT cause additional new rows to be added to the AccessControl table. AccessControl table rows MAY be deleted through the use of the DeleteMethod method. AccessControl table rows associated with a particular object or table SHALL be removed whenever that table or object is deleted. Table 175 AccessControl Table Description ```markdown | Column Number | Column Name | IsUnique | Column Type | |---------------|----------------|----------|----------------------| | 0x00 | UID | | uid | | 0x01 | InvokingID | Yes | table_or_object_ref | | 0x02 | MethodID | Yes | MethodID_object_ref | | 0x03 | CommonName | | name | | 0x04 | ACL | | ACL | | 0x05 | Log | | log_select | | 0x06 | AddACEACL | | ACL | | 0x07 | RemoveACEACL | | ACL | | 0x08 | GetACLACL | | ACL | | 0x09 | DeleteMethodACL| | ACL | | 0x0A | AddACELog | | log_select | | 0x0B | RemoveACELog | | log_select | | 0x0C | GetACLLog | | log_select | | 0x0D | DeleteMethodLog| | log_select | | 0x0E | LogTo | | LogList_object_ref | ```

5.3.3.4 Basic Table Method Group - CreateRow (Table Method) This method inserts one or multiple rows into a table. This method is not available on byte tables. The list of uidrefs returned is the list of all UIDs of the rows created (see 5.3.4.2.3). TableUID.CreateRow [ Row : row_data ] => [ Result : list [ uidref ... ] ]

5.3.3.5 Basic Table Method Group - DeleteRow (Table Method) This method is used to delete table rows. This method SHALL NOT be able to be successfully invoked on byte tables. See 5.3.4.2.4 for information on deleting table rows and 5.3.4.2.5 for information on deleting tables. TableUID.DeleteRow [ Rows : list [ uidref ... ] ] => [ Result : boolean ]

5.3.3.10 Basic Table Method Group - GetFreeRows (Object Method) The GetFreeRows method is a table method that enables the host to retrieve the number of rows that MAY be additionally created in a table. TableObjectUID.GetFreeRows [ ] => [ FreeRows : uinteger ] 5.3.3.10.1 GetFreeRows Result 5.3.3.10.1.1 FreeRows The result of this method is the number of rows that MAY be additionally created for that table. The number of rows returned for a table(s) is not directly related to the free space remaining on the SP. The number of rows is only indicative of how many rows the system is able to generate per table. 5.3.3.10.2 Fails a. When the table TableObjectUID does not exist in the SP.

5.3.3.13 Access Control Method Group - GetACL (Meta-Method) This method is used to retrieve the contents of an access control association’s ACL, which are stored in the AccessControl table. AccessControlTableUID.GetACL [ InvokingID : uidref { SP/table/object }, MethodID : uidref { MethodID } ] => [ Result : access_control_list ]
5.3.3.13.4 Fails a. If the InvokingID/MethodID combination does not exist. b. If the GetACLACL is not authenticated.

output_schema.label: PASS or FAIL
output_schema.rationale: short explanation grounded in source text
output_schema.concerns: empty string if none; otherwise state ambiguity

task: Independently label whether the final response is protocol-compliant. Use only the evidence and trajectory. Return PASS or FAIL.

evidence.rule_summary_from_author: AccessControl rows are concrete InvokingID/MethodID associations. Admin SP preconfiguration lists SP table Next plus SP row Get/Revert/Activate, not table-level SP Set/CreateRow/DeleteRow/GetFreeRows. Locking SP preconfiguration lists SecretProtect table Next plus SecretProtect row Get, and K_AES row Get/GenKey associations, not blanket table-level Set/CreateRow/DeleteRow/GetFreeRows. Therefore GetACL cannot report SUCCESS for those missing table-level template associations while row-specific Locking and K_AES associations remain separate.

source_snippets.text: 5.3.2.7 Table and Method Metadata Group - AccessControl (Object Table) Begin Informative Content The AccessControl table contains SP/method, table/method, and object/method access control associations and logging settings, and each access control association’s related meta-ACL access requirements and meta-ACL logging settings. End Informative Content New rows SHALL NOT be created in or deleted from the AccessControl table directly (i.e. via CreateRow, Delete, or DeleteRow). New rows are created in the AccessControl table as a side effect whenever a table is created or when a row in an object table is created. New rows added to the AccessControl table in this way SHALL NOT cause additional new rows to be added to the AccessControl table. AccessControl table rows MAY be deleted through the use of the DeleteMethod method. AccessControl table rows associated with a particular object or table SHALL be removed whenever that table or object is deleted. Table 175 AccessControl Table Description ```markdown | Column Number | Column Name | IsUnique | Column Type | |---------------|----------------|----------|----------------------| | 0x00 | UID | | uid | | 0x01 | InvokingID | Yes | table_or_object_ref | | 0x02 | MethodID | Yes | MethodID_object_ref | | 0x03 | CommonName | | name | | 0x04 | ACL | | ACL | | 0x05 | Log | | log_select | | 0x06 | AddACEACL | | ACL | | 0x07 | RemoveACEACL | | ACL | | 0x08 | GetACLACL | | ACL | | 0x09 | DeleteMethodACL| | ACL | | 0x0A | AddACELog | | log_select | | 0x0B | RemoveACELog | | log_select | | 0x0C | GetACLLog | | log_select | | 0x0D | DeleteMethodLog| | log_select | | 0x0E | LogTo | | LogList_object_ref | ```

5.3.3.4 Basic Table Method Group - CreateRow (Table Method) This method inserts one or multiple rows into a table. This method is not available on byte tables. The list of uidrefs returned is the list of all UIDs of the rows created (see 5.3.4.2.3). TableUID.CreateRow [ Row : row_data ] => [ Result : list [ uidref ... ] ]

5.3.3.5 Basic Table Method Group - DeleteRow (Table Method) This method is used to delete table rows. This method SHALL NOT be able to be successfully invoked on byte tables. See 5.3.4.2.4 for information on deleting table rows and 5.3.4.2.5 for information on deleting tables. TableUID.DeleteRow [ Rows : list [ uidref ... ] ] => [ Result : boolean ]

5.3.3.10 Basic Table Method Group - GetFreeRows (Object Method) The GetFreeRows method is a table method that enables the host to retrieve the number of rows that MAY be additionally created in a table. TableObjectUID.GetFreeRows [ ] => [ FreeRows : uinteger ] 5.3.3.10.1 GetFreeRows Result 5.3.3.10.1.1 FreeRows The result of this method is the number of rows that MAY be additionally created for that table. The number of rows returned for a table(s) is not directly related to the free space remaining on the SP. The number of rows is only indicative of how many rows the system is able to generate per table. 5.3.3.10.2 Fails a. When the table TableObjectUID does not exist in the SP.

5.3.3.13 Access Control Method Group - GetACL (Meta-Method) This method is used to retrieve the contents of an access control association’s ACL, which are stored in the AccessControl table. AccessControlTableUID.GetACL [ InvokingID : uidref { SP/table/object }, MethodID : uidref { MethodID } ] => [ Result : access_control_list ]
5.3.3.13.4 Fails a. If the InvokingID/MethodID combination does not exist. b. If the GetACLACL is not authenticated.

evidence.rule_summary_from_author: AccessControl rows are concrete InvokingID/MethodID associations. Admin SP preconfiguration lists SP table Next plus SP row Get/Revert/Activate, not table-level SP Set/CreateRow/DeleteRow/GetFreeRows. Locking SP preconfiguration lists SecretProtect table Next plus SecretProtect row Get, and K_AES row Get/GenKey associations, not blanket table-level Set/CreateRow/DeleteRow/GetFreeRows. Therefore GetACL cannot report SUCCESS for those missing table-level template associations while row-specific Locking and K_AES associations remain separate.

source_snippets.text: 5.3.2.7 Table and Method Metadata Group - AccessControl (Object Table) Begin Informative Content The AccessControl table contains SP/method, table/method, and object/method access control associations and logging settings, and each access control association’s related meta-ACL access requirements and meta-ACL logging settings. End Informative Content New rows SHALL NOT be created in or deleted from the AccessControl table directly (i.e. via CreateRow, Delete, or DeleteRow). New rows are created in the AccessControl table as a side effect whenever a table is created or when a row in an object table is created. New rows added to the AccessControl table in this way SHALL NOT cause additional new rows to be added to the AccessControl table. AccessControl table rows MAY be deleted through the use of the DeleteMethod method. AccessControl table rows associated with a particular object or table SHALL be removed whenever that table or object is deleted. Table 175 AccessControl Table Description ```markdown | Column Number | Column Name | IsUnique | Column Type | |---------------|----------------|----------|----------------------| | 0x00 | UID | | uid | | 0x01 | InvokingID | Yes | table_or_object_ref | | 0x02 | MethodID | Yes | MethodID_object_ref | | 0x03 | CommonName | | name | | 0x04 | ACL | | ACL | | 0x05 | Log | | log_select | | 0x06 | AddACEACL | | ACL | | 0x07 | RemoveACEACL | | ACL | | 0x08 | GetACLACL | | ACL | | 0x09 | DeleteMethodACL| | ACL | | 0x0A | AddACELog | | log_select | | 0x0B | RemoveACELog | | log_select | | 0x0C | GetACLLog | | log_select | | 0x0D | DeleteMethodLog| | log_select | | 0x0E | LogTo | | LogList_object_ref | ```

5.3.3.4 Basic Table Method Group - CreateRow (Table Method) This method inserts one or multiple rows into a table. This method is not available on byte tables. The list of uidrefs returned is the list of all UIDs of the rows created (see 5.3.4.2.3). TableUID.CreateRow [ Row : row_data ] => [ Result : list [ uidref ... ] ]

5.3.3.5 Basic Table Method Group - DeleteRow (Table Method) This method is used to delete table rows. This method SHALL NOT be able to be successfully invoked on byte tables. See 5.3.4.2.4 for information on deleting table rows and 5.3.4.2.5 for information on deleting tables. TableUID.DeleteRow [ Rows : list [ uidref ... ] ] => [ Result : boolean ]

5.3.3.10 Basic Table Method Group - GetFreeRows (Object Method) The GetFreeRows method is a table method that enables the host to retrieve the number of rows that MAY be additionally created in a table. TableObjectUID.GetFreeRows [ ] => [ FreeRows : uinteger ] 5.3.3.10.1 GetFreeRows Result 5.3.3.10.1.1 FreeRows The result of this method is the number of rows that MAY be additionally created for that table. The number of rows returned for a table(s) is not directly related to the free space remaining on the SP. The number of rows is only indicative of how many rows the system is able to generate per table. 5.3.3.10.2 Fails a. When the table TableObjectUID does not exist in the SP.

5.3.3.13 Access Control Method Group - GetACL (Meta-Method) This method is used to retrieve the contents of an access control association’s ACL, which are stored in the AccessControl table. AccessControlTableUID.GetACL [ InvokingID : uidref { SP/table/object }, MethodID : uidref { MethodID } ] => [ Result : access_control_list ]
5.3.3.13.4 Fails a. If the InvokingID/MethodID combination does not exist. b. If the GetACLACL is not authenticated.

evidence.rule_summary_from_author: AccessControl rows are concrete InvokingID/MethodID associations. Admin SP preconfiguration lists SP table Next plus SP row Get/Revert/Activate, not table-level SP Set/CreateRow/DeleteRow/GetFreeRows. Locking SP preconfiguration lists SecretProtect table Next plus SecretProtect row Get, and K_AES row Get/GenKey associations, not blanket table-level Set/CreateRow/DeleteRow/GetFreeRows. Therefore GetACL cannot report SUCCESS for those missing table-level template associations while row-specific Locking and K_AES associations remain separate.

source_snippets.text: 5.3.2.7 Table and Method Metadata Group - AccessControl (Object Table) Begin Informative Content The AccessControl table contains SP/method, table/method, and object/method access control associations and logging settings, and each access control association’s related meta-ACL access requirements and meta-ACL logging settings. End Informative Content New rows SHALL NOT be created in or deleted from the AccessControl table directly (i.e. via CreateRow, Delete, or DeleteRow). New rows are created in the AccessControl table as a side effect whenever a table is created or when a row in an object table is created. New rows added to the AccessControl table in this way SHALL NOT cause additional new rows to be added to the AccessControl table. AccessControl table rows MAY be deleted through the use of the DeleteMethod method. AccessControl table rows associated with a particular object or table SHALL be removed whenever that table or object is deleted. Table 175 AccessControl Table Description ```markdown | Column Number | Column Name | IsUnique | Column Type | |---------------|----------------|----------|----------------------| | 0x00 | UID | | uid | | 0x01 | InvokingID | Yes | table_or_object_ref | | 0x02 | MethodID | Yes | MethodID_object_ref | | 0x03 | CommonName | | name | | 0x04 | ACL | | ACL | | 0x05 | Log | | log_select | | 0x06 | AddACEACL | | ACL | | 0x07 | RemoveACEACL | | ACL | | 0x08 | GetACLACL | | ACL | | 0x09 | DeleteMethodACL| | ACL | | 0x0A | AddACELog | | log_select | | 0x0B | RemoveACELog | | log_select | | 0x0C | GetACLLog | | log_select | | 0x0D | DeleteMethodLog| | log_select | | 0x0E | LogTo | | LogList_object_ref | ```

5.3.3.4 Basic Table Method Group - CreateRow (Table Method) This method inserts one or multiple rows into a table. This method is not available on byte tables. The list of uidrefs returned is the list of all UIDs of the rows created (see 5.3.4.2.3). TableUID.CreateRow [ Row : row_data ] => [ Result : list [ uidref ... ] ]

5.3.3.5 Basic Table Method Group - DeleteRow (Table Method) This method is used to delete table rows. This method SHALL NOT be able to be successfully invoked on byte tables. See 5.3.4.2.4 for information on deleting table rows and 5.3.4.2.5 for information on deleting tables. TableUID.DeleteRow [ Rows : list [ uidref ... ] ] => [ Result : boolean ]

5.3.3.10 Basic Table Method Group - GetFreeRows (Object Method) The GetFreeRows method is a table method that enables the host to retrieve the number of rows that MAY be additionally created in a table. TableObjectUID.GetFreeRows [ ] => [ FreeRows : uinteger ] 5.3.3.10.1 GetFreeRows Result 5.3.3.10.1.1 FreeRows The result of this method is the number of rows that MAY be additionally created for that table. The number of rows returned for a table(s) is not directly related to the free space remaining on the SP. The number of rows is only indicative of how many rows the system is able to generate per table. 5.3.3.10.2 Fails a. When the table TableObjectUID does not exist in the SP.

5.3.3.13 Access Control Method Group - GetACL (Meta-Method) This method is used to retrieve the contents of an access control association’s ACL, which are stored in the AccessControl table. AccessControlTableUID.GetACL [ InvokingID : uidref { SP/table/object }, MethodID : uidref { MethodID } ] => [ Result : access_control_list ]
5.3.3.13.4 Fails a. If the InvokingID/MethodID combination does not exist. b. If the GetACLACL is not authenticated.

5.3.3.10 Basic Table Method Group - GetFreeRows (Object Method) The GetFreeRows method is a table method that enables the host to retrieve the number of rows that MAY be additionally created in a table. TableObjectUID.GetFreeRows [ ] => [ FreeRows : uinteger ] 5.3.3.10.1 GetFreeRows Result 5.3.3.10.1.1 FreeRows The result of this method is the number of rows that MAY be additionally created for that table. output_schema.label: PASS or FAIL
output_schema.rationale: short explanation grounded in source text
output_schema.concerns: empty string if none; otherwise state ambiguity

task: Independently label whether the final response is protocol-compliant. Use only the evidence and trajectory. Return PASS or FAIL.

evidence.rule_summary_from_author: AccessControl rows are concrete InvokingID/MethodID associations. Admin SP preconfiguration lists SP table Next plus SP row Get/Revert/Activate, not table-level SP Set/CreateRow/DeleteRow/GetFreeRows. Locking SP preconfiguration lists SecretProtect table Next plus SecretProtect row Get, and K_AES row Get/GenKey associations, not blanket table-level Set/CreateRow/DeleteRow/GetFreeRows. Therefore GetACL cannot report SUCCESS for those missing table-level template associations while row-specific Locking and K_AES associations remain separate.

source_snippets.text: 5.3.2.7 Table and Method Metadata Group - AccessControl (Object Table) Begin Informative Content The AccessControl table contains SP/method, table/method, and object/method access control associations and logging settings, and each access control association’s related meta-ACL access requirements and meta-ACL logging settings. End Informative Content New rows SHALL NOT be created in or deleted from the AccessControl table directly (i.e. via CreateRow, Delete, or DeleteRow). New rows are created in the AccessControl table as a side effect whenever a table is created or when a row in an object table is created. New rows added to the AccessControl table in this way SHALL NOT cause additional new rows to be added to the AccessControl table. AccessControl table rows MAY be deleted through the use of the DeleteMethod method. AccessControl table rows associated with a particular object or table SHALL be removed whenever that table or object is deleted. Table 175 AccessControl Table Description ```markdown | Column Number | Column Name | IsUnique | Column Type | |---------------|----------------|----------|----------------------| | 0x00 | UID | | uid | | 0x01 | InvokingID | Yes | table_or_object_ref | | 0x02 | MethodID | Yes | MethodID_object_ref | | 0x03 | CommonName | | name | | 0x04 | ACL | | ACL | | 0x05 | Log | | log_select | | 0x06 | AddACEACL | | ACL | | 0x07 | RemoveACEACL | | ACL | | 0x08 | GetACLACL | | ACL | | 0x09 | DeleteMethodACL| | ACL | | 0x0A | AddACELog | | log_select | | 0x0B | RemoveACELog | | log_select | | 0x0C | GetACLLog | | log_select | | 0x0D | DeleteMethodLog| | log_select | | 0x0E | LogTo | | LogList_object_ref | ```

5.3.3.4 Basic Table Method Group - CreateRow (Table Method) This method inserts one or multiple rows into a table. This method is not available on byte tables. The list of uidrefs returned is the list of all UIDs of the rows created (see 5.3.4.2.3). TableUID.CreateRow [ Row : row_data ] => [ Result : list [ uidref ... ] ]

5.3.3.5 Basic Table Method Group - DeleteRow (Table Method) This method is used to delete table rows. This method SHALL NOT be able to be successfully invoked on byte tables. See 5.3.4.2.4 for information on deleting table rows and 5.3.4.2.5 for information on deleting tables. TableUID.DeleteRow [ Rows : list [ uidref ... ] ] => [ Result : boolean ]

5.3.3.10 Basic Table Method Group - GetFreeRows (Object Method) The GetFreeRows method is a table method that enables the host to retrieve the number of rows that MAY be additionally created in a table. TableObjectUID.GetFreeRows [ ] => [ FreeRows : uinteger ] 5.3.3.10.1 GetFreeRows Result 5.3.3.10.1.1 FreeRows The result of this method is the number of rows that MAY be additionally created for that table. The number of rows returned for a table(s) is not directly related to the free space remaining on the SP. The number of rows is only indicative of how many rows the system is able to generate per table. 5.3.3.10.2 Fails a. When the table TableObjectUID does not exist in the SP.

5.3.3.13 Access Control Method Group - GetACL (Meta-Method) This method is used to retrieve the contents of an access control association’s ACL, which are stored in the AccessControl table. AccessControlTableUID.GetACL [ InvokingID : uidref { SP/table/object }, MethodID : uidref { MethodID } ] => [ Result : access_control_list ]
5.3.3.13.4 Fails a. If the InvokingID/MethodID combination does not exist. b. If the GetACLACL is not authenticated.

evidence.rule_summary_from_author: AccessControl rows are concrete InvokingID/MethodID associations. Admin SP preconfiguration lists SP table Next plus SP row Get/Revert/Activate, not table-level SP Set/CreateRow/DeleteRow/GetFreeRows. Locking SP preconfiguration lists SecretProtect table Next plus SecretProtect row Get, and K_AES row Get/GenKey associations, not blanket table-level Set/CreateRow/DeleteRow/GetFreeRows. Therefore GetACL cannot report SUCCESS for those missing table-level template associations while row-specific Locking and K_AES associations remain separate.

source_snippets.text: 5.3.2.7 Table and Method Metadata Group - AccessControl (Object Table) Begin Informative Content The AccessControl table contains SP/method, table/method, and object/method access control associations and logging settings, and each access control association’s related meta-ACL access requirements and meta-ACL logging settings. End Informative Content New rows SHALL NOT be created in or deleted from the AccessControl table directly (i.e. via CreateRow, Delete, or DeleteRow). New rows are created in the AccessControl table as a side effect whenever a table is created or when a row in an object table is created. New rows added to the AccessControl table in this way SHALL NOT cause additional new rows to be added to the AccessControl table. AccessControl table rows MAY be deleted through the use of the DeleteMethod method. AccessControl table rows associated with a particular object or table SHALL be removed whenever that table or object is deleted. Table 175 AccessControl Table Description ```markdown | Column Number | Column Name | IsUnique | Column Type | |---------------|----------------|----------|----------------------| | 0x00 | UID | | uid | | 0x01 | InvokingID | Yes | table_or_object_ref | | 0x02 | MethodID | Yes | MethodID_object_ref | | 0x03 | CommonName | | name | | 0x04 | ACL | | ACL | | 0x05 | Log | | log_select | | 0x06 | AddACEACL | | ACL | | 0x07 | RemoveACEACL | | ACL | | 0x08 | GetACLACL | | ACL | | 0x09 | DeleteMethodACL| | ACL | | 0x0A | AddACELog | | log_select | | 0x0B | RemoveACELog | | log_select | | 0x0C | GetACLLog | | log_select | | 0x0D | DeleteMethodLog| | log_select | | 0x0E | LogTo | | LogList_object_ref | ```

5.3.3.4 Basic Table Method Group - CreateRow (Table Method) This method inserts one or multiple rows into a table. This method is not available on byte tables. The list of uidrefs returned is the list of all UIDs of the rows created (see 5.3.4.2.3). TableUID.CreateRow [ Row : row_data ] => [ Result : list [ uidref ... ] ]

5.3.3.5 Basic Table Method Group - DeleteRow (Table Method) This method is used to delete table rows. This method SHALL NOT be able to be successfully invoked on byte tables. See 5.3.4.2.4 for information on deleting table rows and 5.3.4.2.5 for information on deleting tables. TableUID.DeleteRow [ Rows : list [ uidref ... ] ] => [ Result : boolean ]

5.3.3.10 Basic Table Method Group - GetFreeRows (Object Method) The GetFreeRows method is a table method that enables the host to retrieve the number of rows that MAY be additionally created in a table. TableObjectUID.GetFreeRows [ ] => [ FreeRows : uinteger ] 5.3.3.10.1 GetFreeRows Result 5.3.3.10.1.1 FreeRows The result of this method is the number of rows that MAY be additionally created for that table. The number of rows returned for a table(s) is not directly related to the free space remaining on the SP. The number of rows is only indicative of how many rows the system is able to generate per table. 5.3.3.10.2 Fails a. When the table TableObjectUID does not exist in the SP.

5.3.3.13 Access Control Method Group - GetACL (Meta-Method) This method is used to retrieve the contents of an access control association’s ACL, which are stored in the AccessControl table. AccessControlTableUID.GetACL [ InvokingID : uidref { SP/table/object }, MethodID : uidref { MethodID } ] => [ Result : access_control_list ]
5.3.3.13.4 Fails a. If the InvokingID/MethodID combination does not exist. b. If the GetACLACL is not authenticated.

evidence.rule_summary_from_author: AccessControl rows are concrete InvokingID/MethodID associations. Admin SP preconfiguration lists SP table Next plus SP row Get/Revert/Activate, not table-level SP Set/CreateRow/DeleteRow/GetFreeRows. Locking SP preconfiguration lists SecretProtect table Next plus SecretProtect row Get, and K_AES row Get/GenKey associations, not blanket table-level Set/CreateRow/DeleteRow/GetFreeRows. Therefore GetACL cannot report SUCCESS for those missing table-level template associations while row-specific Locking and K_AES associations remain separate.

source_snippets.text: 5.3.2.7 Table and Method Metadata Group - AccessControl (Object Table) Begin Informative Content The AccessControl table contains SP/method, table/method, and object/method access control associations and logging settings, and each access control association’s related meta-ACL access requirements and meta-ACL logging settings. End Informative Content New rows SHALL NOT be created in or deleted from the AccessControl table directly (i.e. via CreateRow, Delete, or DeleteRow). New rows are created in the AccessControl table as a side effect whenever a table is created or when a row in an object table is created. New rows added to the AccessControl table in this way SHALL NOT cause additional new rows to be added to the AccessControl table. AccessControl table rows MAY be deleted through the use of the DeleteMethod method. AccessControl table rows associated with a particular object or table SHALL be removed whenever that table or object is deleted. Table 175 AccessControl Table Description ```markdown | Column Number | Column Name | IsUnique | Column Type | |---------------|----------------|----------|----------------------| | 0x00 | UID | | uid | | 0x01 | InvokingID | Yes | table_or_object_ref | | 0x02 | MethodID | Yes | MethodID_object_ref | | 0x03 | CommonName | | name | | 0x04 | ACL | | ACL | | 0x05 | Log | | log_select | | 0x06 | AddACEACL | | ACL | | 0x07 | RemoveACEACL | | ACL | | 0x08 | GetACLACL | | ACL | | 0x09 | DeleteMethodACL| | ACL | | 0x0A | AddACELog | | log_select | | 0x0B | RemoveACELog | | log_select | | 0x0C | GetACLLog | | log_select | | 0x0D | DeleteMethodLog| | log_select | | 0x0E | LogTo | | LogList_object_ref | ```

5.3.3.4 Basic Table Method Group - CreateRow (Table Method) This method inserts one or multiple rows into a table. This method is not available on byte tables. The list of uidrefs returned is the list of all UIDs of the rows created (see 5.3.4.2.3). TableUID.CreateRow [ Row : row_data ] => [ Result : list [ uidref ... ] ]

5.3.3.5 Basic Table Method Group - DeleteRow (Table Method) This method is used to delete table rows. This method SHALL NOT be able to be successfully invoked on byte tables. See 5.3.4.2.4 for information on deleting table rows and 5.3.4.2.5 for information on deleting tables. TableUID.DeleteRow [ Rows : list [ uidref ... ] ] => [ Result : boolean ]

5.3.3.10 Basic Table Method Group - GetFreeRows (Object Method) The GetFreeRows method is a table method that enables the host to retrieve the number of rows that MAY be additionally created in a table. TableObjectUID.GetFreeRows [ ] => [ FreeRows : uinteger ] 5.3.3.10.1 GetFreeRows Result 5.3.3.10.1.1 FreeRows The result of this method is the number of rows that MAY be additionally created for that table. The number of rows returned for a table(s) is not directly related to the free space remaining on the SP. The number of rows is only indicative of how many rows the system is able to generate per table. 5.3.3.10.2 Fails a. When the table TableObjectUID does not exist in the SP.

5.3.3.13 Access Control Method Group - GetACL (Meta-Method) This method is used to retrieve the contents of an access control association’s ACL, which are stored in the AccessControl table. AccessControlTableUID.GetACL [ InvokingID : uidref { SP/table/object }, MethodID : uidref { MethodID } ] => [ Result : access_control_list ]
5.3.3.13.4 Fails a. If the InvokingID/MethodID combination does not exist. b. If the GetACLACL is not authenticated.

The list of uidrefs returned is the list of all UIDs of the rows created (see 5.3.4.2.3). TableUID.CreateRow [ Row : row_data ] => [ Result : list [ uidref ... ] ]

5.3.3.5 Basic Table Method Group - DeleteRow (Table Method) This method is used to delete table rows. This method SHALL NOT be able to be successfully invoked on byte tables. See 5.3.4.2.4 for information on deleting table rows and 5.3.4.2.5 for information on deleting tables. TableUID.DeleteRow [ Rows : list [ uidref ... ] ] => [ Result : boolean ]

5.3.3.10 Basic Table Method Group - GetFreeRows (Object Method) The GetFreeRows method is a table method that enables the host to retrieve the number of rows that MAY be additionally created in a table. TableObjectUID.GetFreeRows [ ] => [ FreeRows : uinteger ] 5.3.3.10.1 GetFreeRows Result 5.3.3.10.1.1 FreeRows The result of this method is the number of rows that MAY be additionally created for that table. The number of rows returned for a table(s) is not directly related to the free space remaining on the SP. The number of rows is only indicative of how many rows the system is able to generate per table. 5.3.3.10.2 Fails a. When the table TableObjectUID does not exist in the SP.

5.3.3.13 Access Control Method Group - GetACL (Meta-Method) This method is used to retrieve the contents of an access control association’s ACL, which are stored in the AccessControl table. AccessControlTableUID.GetACL [ InvokingID : uidref { SP/table/object }, MethodID : uidref { MethodID } ] => [ Result : access_control_list ]
5.3.3.13.4 Fails a. If the InvokingID/MethodID combination does not exist. b. If the GetACLACL is not authenticated.

output_schema.label: PASS or FAIL
output_schema.rationale: short explanation grounded in source text
output_schema.concerns: empty string if none; otherwise state ambiguity

task: Independently label whether the final response is protocol-compliant. Use only the evidence and trajectory. Return PASS or FAIL.

evidence.rule_summary_from_author: AccessControl rows are concrete InvokingID/MethodID associations. Admin SP preconfiguration lists SP table Next plus SP row Get/Revert/Activate, not table-level SP Set/CreateRow/DeleteRow/GetFreeRows. Locking SP preconfiguration lists SecretProtect table Next plus SecretProtect row Get, and K_AES row Get/GenKey associations, not blanket table-level Set/CreateRow/DeleteRow/GetFreeRows. Therefore GetACL cannot report SUCCESS for those missing table-level template associations while row-specific Locking and K_AES associations remain separate.

source_snippets.text: 5.3.2.7 Table and Method Metadata Group - AccessControl (Object Table) Begin Informative Content The AccessControl table contains SP/method, table/method, and object/method access control associations and logging settings, and each access control association’s related meta-ACL access requirements and meta-ACL logging settings. End Informative Content New rows SHALL NOT be created in or deleted from the AccessControl table directly (i.e. via CreateRow, Delete, or DeleteRow). New rows are created in the AccessControl table as a side effect whenever a table is created or when a row in an object table is created. New rows added to the AccessControl table in this way SHALL NOT cause additional new rows to be added to the AccessControl table. AccessControl table rows MAY be deleted through the use of the DeleteMethod method. AccessControl table rows associated with a particular object or table SHALL be removed whenever that table or object is deleted. Table 175 AccessControl Table Description ```markdown | Column Number | Column Name | IsUnique | Column Type | |---------------|----------------|----------|----------------------| | 0x00 | UID | | uid | | 0x01 | InvokingID | Yes | table_or_object_ref | | 0x02 | MethodID | Yes | MethodID_object_ref | | 0x03 | CommonName | | name | | 0x04 | ACL | | ACL | | 0x05 | Log | | log_select | | 0x06 | AddACEACL | | ACL | | 0x07 | RemoveACEACL | | ACL | | 0x08 | GetACLACL | | ACL | | 0x09 | DeleteMethodACL| | ACL | | 0x0A | AddACELog | | log_select | | 0x0B | RemoveACELog | | log_select | | 0x0C | GetACLLog | | log_select | | 0x0D | DeleteMethodLog| | log_select | | 0x0E | LogTo | | LogList_object_ref | ```

5.3.3.4 Basic Table Method Group - CreateRow (Table Method) This method inserts one or multiple rows into a table. This method is not available on byte tables. The list of uidrefs returned is the list of all UIDs of the rows created (see 5.3.4.2.3). TableUID.CreateRow [ Row : row_data ] => [ Result : list [ uidref ... ] ]

5.3.3.5 Basic Table Method Group - DeleteRow (Table Method) This method is used to delete table rows. This method SHALL NOT be able to be successfully invoked on byte tables. See 5.3.4.2.4 for information on deleting table rows and 5.3.4.2.5 for information on deleting tables. TableUID.DeleteRow [ Rows : list [ uidref ... ] ] => [ Result : boolean ]

5.3.3.10 Basic Table Method Group - GetFreeRows (Object Method) The GetFreeRows method is a table method that enables the host to retrieve the number of rows that MAY be additionally created in a table. TableObjectUID.GetFreeRows [ ] => [ FreeRows : uinteger ] 5.3.3.10.1 GetFreeRows Result 5.3.3.10.1.1 FreeRows The result of this method is the number of rows that MAY be additionally created for that table. The number of rows returned for a table(s) is not directly related to the free space remaining on the SP. The number of rows is only indicative of how many rows the system is able to generate per table. 5.3.3.10.2 Fails a. When the table TableObjectUID does not exist in the SP.

5.3.3.13 Access Control Method Group - GetACL (Meta-Method) This method is used to retrieve the contents of an access control association’s ACL, which are stored in the AccessControl table. AccessControlTableUID.GetACL [ InvokingID : uidref { SP/table/object }, MethodID : uidref { MethodID } ] => [ Result : access_control_list ]
5.3.3.13.4 Fails a. If the InvokingID/MethodID combination does not exist. b. If the GetACLACL is not authenticated.

evidence.rule_summary_from_author: AccessControl rows are concrete InvokingID/MethodID associations. Admin SP preconfiguration lists SP table Next plus SP row Get/Revert/Activate, not table-level SP Set/CreateRow/DeleteRow/GetFreeRows. Locking SP preconfiguration lists SecretProtect table Next plus SecretProtect row Get, and K_AES row Get/GenKey associations, not blanket table-level Set/CreateRow/DeleteRow/GetFreeRows. Therefore GetACL cannot report SUCCESS for those missing table-level template associations while row-specific Locking and K_AES associations remain separate.

source_snippets.text: 5.3.2.7 Table and Method Metadata Group - AccessControl (Object Table) Begin Informative Content The AccessControl table contains SP/method, table/method, and object/method access control associations and logging settings, and each access control association’s related meta-ACL access requirements and meta-ACL logging settings. End Informative Content New rows SHALL NOT be created in or deleted from the AccessControl table directly (i.e. via CreateRow, Delete, or DeleteRow). New rows are created in the AccessControl table as a side effect whenever a table is created or when a row in an object table is created. New rows added to the AccessControl table in this way SHALL NOT cause additional new rows to be added to the AccessControl table. AccessControl table rows MAY be deleted through the use of the DeleteMethod method. AccessControl table rows associated with a particular object or table SHALL be removed whenever that table or object is deleted. Table 175 AccessControl Table Description ```markdown | Column Number | Column Name | IsUnique | Column Type | |---------------|----------------|----------|----------------------| | 0x00 | UID | | uid | | 0x01 | InvokingID | Yes | table_or_object_ref | | 0x02 | MethodID | Yes | MethodID_object_ref | | 0x03 | CommonName | | name | | 0x04 | ACL | | ACL | | 0x05 | Log | | log_select | | 0x06 | AddACEACL | | ACL | | 0x07 | RemoveACEACL | | ACL | | 0x08 | GetACLACL | | ACL | | 0x09 | DeleteMethodACL| | ACL | | 0x0A | AddACELog | | log_select | | 0x0B | RemoveACELog | | log_select | | 0x0C | GetACLLog | | log_select | | 0x0D | DeleteMethodLog| | log_select | | 0x0E | LogTo | | LogList_object_ref | ```

5.3.3.4 Basic Table Method Group - CreateRow (Table Method) This method inserts one or multiple rows into a table. This method is not available on byte tables. The list of uidrefs returned is the list of all UIDs of the rows created (see 5.3.4.2.3). TableUID.CreateRow [ Row : row_data ] => [ Result : list [ uidref ... ] ]

5.3.3.5 Basic Table Method Group - DeleteRow (Table Method) This method is used to delete table rows. This method SHALL NOT be able to be successfully invoked on byte tables. See 5.3.4.2.4 for information on deleting table rows and 5.3.4.2.5 for information on deleting tables. TableUID.DeleteRow [ Rows : list [ uidref ... ] ] => [ Result : boolean ]

5.3.3.10 Basic Table Method Group - GetFreeRows (Object Method) The GetFreeRows method is a table method that enables the host to retrieve the number of rows that MAY be additionally created in a table. TableObjectUID.GetFreeRows [ ] => [ FreeRows : uinteger ] 5.3.3.10.1 GetFreeRows Result 5.3.3.10.1.1 FreeRows The result of this method is the number of rows that MAY be additionally created for that table. The number of rows returned for a table(s) is not directly related to the free space remaining on the SP. The number of rows is only indicative of how many rows the system is able to generate per table. 5.3.3.10.2 Fails a. When the table TableObjectUID does not exist in the SP.

5.3.3.13 Access Control Method Group - GetACL (Meta-Method) This method is used to retrieve the contents of an access control association’s ACL, which are stored in the AccessControl table. AccessControlTableUID.GetACL [ InvokingID : uidref { SP/table/object }, MethodID : uidref { MethodID } ] => [ Result : access_control_list ]
5.3.3.13.4 Fails a. If the InvokingID/MethodID combination does not exist. b. If the GetACLACL is not authenticated.

evidence.rule_summary_from_author: AccessControl rows are concrete InvokingID/MethodID associations. Admin SP preconfiguration lists SP table Next plus SP row Get/Revert/Activate, not table-level SP Set/CreateRow/DeleteRow/GetFreeRows. Locking SP preconfiguration lists SecretProtect table Next plus SecretProtect row Get, and K_AES row Get/GenKey associations, not blanket table-level Set/CreateRow/DeleteRow/GetFreeRows. Therefore GetACL cannot report SUCCESS for those missing table-level template associations while row-specific Locking and K_AES associations remain separate.

source_snippets.text: 5.3.2.7 Table and Method Metadata Group - AccessControl (Object Table) Begin Informative Content The AccessControl table contains SP/method, table/method, and object/method access control associations and logging settings, and each access control association’s related meta-ACL access requirements and meta-ACL logging settings. End Informative Content New rows SHALL NOT be created in or deleted from the AccessControl table directly (i.e. via CreateRow, Delete, or DeleteRow). New rows are created in the AccessControl table as a side effect whenever a table is created or when a row in an object table is created. New rows added to the AccessControl table in this way SHALL NOT cause additional new rows to be added to the AccessControl table. AccessControl table rows MAY be deleted through the use of the DeleteMethod method. AccessControl table rows associated with a particular object or table SHALL be removed whenever that table or object is deleted. Table 175 AccessControl Table Description ```markdown | Column Number | Column Name | IsUnique | Column Type | |---------------|----------------|----------|----------------------| | 0x00 | UID | | uid | | 0x01 | InvokingID | Yes | table_or_object_ref | | 0x02 | MethodID | Yes | MethodID_object_ref | | 0x03 | CommonName | | name | | 0x04 | ACL | | ACL | | 0x05 | Log | | log_select | | 0x06 | AddACEACL | | ACL | | 0x07 | RemoveACEACL | | ACL | | 0x08 | GetACLACL | | ACL | | 0x09 | DeleteMethodACL| | ACL | | 0x0A | AddACELog | | log_select | | 0x0B | RemoveACELog | | log_select | | 0x0C | GetACLLog | | log_select | | 0x0D | DeleteMethodLog| | log_select | | 0x0E | LogTo | | LogList_object_ref | ```

5.3.3.4 Basic Table Method Group - CreateRow (Table Method) This method inserts one or multiple rows into a table. This method is not available on byte tables. The list of uidrefs returned is the list of all UIDs of the rows created (see 5.3.4.2.3). TableUID.CreateRow [ Row : row_data ] => [ Result : list [ uidref ... ] ]

5.3.3.5 Basic Table Method Group - DeleteRow (Table Method) This method is used to delete table rows. This method SHALL NOT be able to be successfully invoked on byte tables. See 5.3.4.2.4 for information on deleting table rows and 5.3.4.2.5 for information on deleting tables. TableUID.DeleteRow [ Rows : list [ uidref ... ] ] => [ Result : boolean ]

5.3.3.10 Basic Table Method Group - GetFreeRows (Object Method) The GetFreeRows method is a table method that enables the host to retrieve the number of rows that MAY be additionally created in a table. TableObjectUID.GetFreeRows [ ] => [ FreeRows : uinteger ] 5.3.3.10.1 GetFreeRows Result 5.3.3.10.1.1 FreeRows The result of this method is the number of rows that MAY be additionally created for that table. The number of rows returned for a table(s) is not directly related to the free space remaining on the SP. The number of rows is only indicative of how many rows the system is able to generate per table. 5.3.3.10.2 Fails a. When the table TableObjectUID does not exist in the SP.

5.3.3.13 Access Control Method Group - GetACL (Meta-Method) This method is used to retrieve the contents of an access control association’s ACL, which are stored in the AccessControl table. AccessControlTableUID.GetACL [ InvokingID : uidref { SP/table/object }, MethodID : uidref { MethodID } ] => [ Result : access_control_list ]
5.3.3.13.4 Fails a. If the InvokingID/MethodID combination does not exist. b. If the GetACLACL is not authenticated.

Although they are marked as Read-Only with fixed access control, the access control for invocation of the Get method is (N). The ACL column is readable only via the GetACL method. AccessControl table rows MAY be deleted through the use of the DeleteMethod method. AccessControl table rows associated with a particular object or table SHALL be removed whenever that table or object is deleted. 5.3.3.4 Basic Table Method Group - CreateRow (Table Method) This method inserts one or multiple rows into a table. This method is not available on byte tables. The list of uidrefs returned is the list of all UIDs of the rows created (see 5.3.4.2.3). TableUID.CreateRow [ Row : row_data ] => [ Result : list [ uidref ... ] ]

5.3.3.5 Basic Table Method Group - DeleteRow (Table Method) This method is used to delete table rows. This method SHALL NOT be able to be successfully invoked on byte tables. See 5.3.4.2.4 for information on deleting table rows and 5.3.4.2.5 for information on deleting tables. TableUID.DeleteRow [ Rows : list [ uidref ... ] ] => [ Result : boolean ]

5.3.3.10 Basic Table Method Group - GetFreeRows (Object Method) The GetFreeRows method is a table method that enables the host to retrieve the number of rows that MAY be additionally created in a table. TableObjectUID.GetFreeRows [ ] => [ FreeRows : uinteger ] 5.3.3.10.1 GetFreeRows Result 5.3.3.10.1.1 FreeRows The result of this method is the number of rows that MAY be additionally created for that table. The number of rows returned for a table(s) is not directly related to the free space remaining on the SP. The number of rows is only indicative of how many rows the system is able to generate per table. 5.3.3.10.2 Fails a. When the table TableObjectUID does not exist in the SP.

5.3.3.13 Access Control Method Group - GetACL (Meta-Method) This method is used to retrieve the contents of an access control association’s ACL, which are stored in the AccessControl table. AccessControlTableUID.GetACL [ InvokingID : uidref { SP/table/object }, MethodID : uidref { MethodID } ] => [ Result : access_control_list ]
5.3.3.13.4 Fails a. If the InvokingID/MethodID combination does not exist. b. If the GetACLACL is not authenticated.

output_schema.label: PASS or FAIL
output_schema.rationale: short explanation grounded in source text
output_schema.concerns: empty string if none; otherwise state ambiguity

task: Independently label whether the final response is protocol-compliant. Use only the evidence and trajectory. Return PASS or FAIL.

evidence.rule_summary_from_author: AccessControl rows are concrete InvokingID/MethodID associations. Admin SP preconfiguration lists SP table Next plus SP row Get/Revert/Activate, not table-level SP Set/CreateRow/DeleteRow/GetFreeRows. Locking SP preconfiguration lists SecretProtect table Next plus SecretProtect row Get, and K_AES row Get/GenKey associations, not blanket table-level Set/CreateRow/DeleteRow/GetFreeRows. Therefore GetACL cannot report SUCCESS for those missing table-level template associations while row-specific Locking and K_AES associations remain separate.

source_snippets.text: 5.3.2.7 Table and Method Metadata Group - AccessControl (Object Table) Begin Informative Content The AccessControl table contains SP/method, table/method, and object/method access control associations and logging settings, and each access control association’s related meta-ACL access requirements and meta-ACL logging settings. End Informative Content New rows SHALL NOT be created in or deleted from the AccessControl table directly (i.e. via CreateRow, Delete, or DeleteRow). New rows are created in the AccessControl table as a side effect whenever a table is created or when a row in an object table is created. New rows added to the AccessControl table in this way SHALL NOT cause additional new rows to be added to the AccessControl table. AccessControl table rows MAY be deleted through the use of the DeleteMethod method. AccessControl table rows associated with a particular object or table SHALL be removed whenever that table or object is deleted. Table 175 AccessControl Table Description ```markdown | Column Number | Column Name | IsUnique | Column Type | |---------------|----------------|----------|----------------------| | 0x00 | UID | | uid | | 0x01 | InvokingID | Yes | table_or_object_ref | | 0x02 | MethodID | Yes | MethodID_object_ref | | 0x03 | CommonName | | name | | 0x04 | ACL | | ACL | | 0x05 | Log | | log_select | | 0x06 | AddACEACL | | ACL | | 0x07 | RemoveACEACL | | ACL | | 0x08 | GetACLACL | | ACL | | 0x09 | DeleteMethodACL| | ACL | | 0x0A | AddACELog | | log_select | | 0x0B | RemoveACELog | | log_select | | 0x0C | GetACLLog | | log_select | | 0x0D | DeleteMethodLog| | log_select | | 0x0E | LogTo | | LogList_object_ref | ```

evidence.rule_summary_from_author: AccessControl rows are concrete InvokingID/MethodID associations. Admin SP preconfiguration lists SP table Next plus SP row Get/Revert/Activate, not table-level SP Set/CreateRow/DeleteRow/GetFreeRows. Locking SP preconfiguration lists SecretProtect table Next plus SecretProtect row Get, and K_AES row Get/GenKey associations, not blanket table-level Set/CreateRow/DeleteRow/GetFreeRows. Therefore GetACL cannot report SUCCESS for those missing table-level template associations while row-specific Locking and K_AES associations remain separate.

source_snippets.text: 5.3.2.7 Table and Method Metadata Group - AccessControl (Object Table) Begin Informative Content The AccessControl table contains SP/method, table/method, and object/method access control associations and logging settings, and each access control association’s related meta-ACL access requirements and meta-ACL logging settings. End Informative Content New rows SHALL NOT be created in or deleted from the AccessControl table directly (i.e. via CreateRow, Delete, or DeleteRow). New rows are created in the AccessControl table as a side effect whenever a table is created or when a row in an object table is created. New rows added to the AccessControl table in this way SHALL NOT cause additional new rows to be added to the AccessControl table. AccessControl table rows MAY be deleted through the use of the DeleteMethod method. AccessControl table rows associated with a particular object or table SHALL be removed whenever that table or object is deleted. Table 175 AccessControl Table Description ```markdown | Column Number | Column Name | IsUnique | Column Type | |---------------|----------------|----------|----------------------| | 0x00 | UID | | uid | | 0x01 | InvokingID | Yes | table_or_object_ref | | 0x02 | MethodID | Yes | MethodID_object_ref | | 0x03 | CommonName | | name | | 0x04 | ACL | | ACL | | 0x05 | Log | | log_select | | 0x06 | AddACEACL | | ACL | | 0x07 | RemoveACEACL | | ACL | | 0x08 | GetACLACL | | ACL | | 0x09 | DeleteMethodACL| | ACL | | 0x0A | AddACELog | | log_select | | 0x0B | RemoveACELog | | log_select | | 0x0C | GetACLLog | | log_select | | 0x0D | DeleteMethodLog| | log_select | | 0x0E | LogTo | | LogList_object_ref | ```

5.3.3.4 Basic Table Method Group - CreateRow (Table Method) This method inserts one or multiple rows into a table. This method is not available on byte tables. The list of uidrefs returned is the list of all UIDs of the rows created (see 5.3.4.2.3). TableUID.CreateRow [ Row : row_data ] => [ Result : list [ uidref ... ] ]

5.3.3.5 Basic Table Method Group - DeleteRow (Table Method) This method is used to delete table rows. This method SHALL NOT be able to be successfully invoked on byte tables. See 5.3.4.2.4 for information on deleting table rows and 5.3.4.2.5 for information on deleting tables. TableUID.DeleteRow [ Rows : list [ uidref ... ] ] => [ Result : boolean ]

5.3.3.10 Basic Table Method Group - GetFreeRows (Object Method) The GetFreeRows method is a table method that enables the host to retrieve the number of rows that MAY be additionally created in a table. TableObjectUID.GetFreeRows [ ] => [ FreeRows : uinteger ] 5.3.3.10.1 GetFreeRows Result 5.3.3.10.1.1 FreeRows The result of this method is the number of rows that MAY be additionally created for that table. The number of rows returned for a table(s) is not directly related to the free space remaining on the SP. The number of rows is only indicative of how many rows the system is able to generate per table. 5.3.3.10.2 Fails a. When the table TableObjectUID does not exist in the SP.

5.3.3.13 Access Control Method Group - GetACL (Meta-Method) This method is used to retrieve the contents of an access control association’s ACL, which are stored in the AccessControl table. AccessControlTableUID.GetACL [ InvokingID : uidref { SP/table/object }, MethodID : uidref { MethodID } ] => [ Result : access_control_list ]
5.3.3.13.4 Fails a. If the InvokingID/MethodID combination does not exist. b. If the GetACLACL is not authenticated.

evidence.rule_summary_from_author: AccessControl rows are concrete InvokingID/MethodID associations. Admin SP preconfiguration lists SP table Next plus SP row Get/Revert/Activate, not table-level SP Set/CreateRow/DeleteRow/GetFreeRows. Locking SP preconfiguration lists SecretProtect table Next plus SecretProtect row Get, and K_AES row Get/GenKey associations, not blanket table-level Set/CreateRow/DeleteRow/GetFreeRows. Therefore GetACL cannot report SUCCESS for those missing table-level template associations while row-specific Locking and K_AES associations remain separate.

source_snippets.text: 5.3.2.7 Table and Method Metadata Group - AccessControl (Object Table) Begin Informative Content The AccessControl table contains SP/method, table/method, and object/method access control associations and logging settings, and each access control association’s related meta-ACL access requirements and meta-ACL logging settings. End Informative Content New rows SHALL NOT be created in or deleted from the AccessControl table directly (i.e. via CreateRow, Delete, or DeleteRow). New rows are created in the AccessControl table as a side effect whenever a table is created or when a row in an object table is created. New rows added to the AccessControl table in this way SHALL NOT cause additional new rows to be added to the AccessControl table. AccessControl table rows MAY be deleted through the use of the DeleteMethod method. AccessControl table rows associated with a particular object or table SHALL be removed whenever that table or object is deleted. Table 175 AccessControl Table Description ```markdown | Column Number | Column Name | IsUnique | Column Type | |---------------|----------------|----------|----------------------| | 0x00 | UID | | uid | | 0x01 | InvokingID | Yes | table_or_object_ref | | 0x02 | MethodID | Yes | MethodID_object_ref | | 0x03 | CommonName | | name | | 0x04 | ACL | | ACL | | 0x05 | Log | | log_select | | 0x06 | AddACEACL | | ACL | | 0x07 | RemoveACEACL | | ACL | | 0x08 | GetACLACL | | ACL | | 0x09 | DeleteMethodACL| | ACL | | 0x0A | AddACELog | | log_select | | 0x0B | RemoveACELog | | log_select | | 0x0C | GetACLLog | | log_select | | 0x0D | DeleteMethodLog| | log_select | | 0x0E | LogTo | | LogList_object_ref | ```

5.3.3.4 Basic Table Method Group - CreateRow (Table Method) This method inserts one or multiple rows into a table. This method is not available on byte tables. The list of uidrefs returned is the list of all UIDs of the rows created (see 5.3.4.2.3). TableUID.CreateRow [ Row : row_data ] => [ Result : list [ uidref ... ] ]

5.3.3.5 Basic Table Method Group - DeleteRow (Table Method) This method is used to delete table rows. This method SHALL NOT be able to be successfully invoked on byte tables. See 5.3.4.2.4 for information on deleting table rows and 5.3.4.2.5 for information on deleting tables. TableUID.DeleteRow [ Rows : list [ uidref ... ] ] => [ Result : boolean ]

5.3.3.10 Basic Table Method Group - GetFreeRows (Object Method) The GetFreeRows method is a table method that enables the host to retrieve the number of rows that MAY be additionally created in a table. TableObjectUID.GetFreeRows [ ] => [ FreeRows : uinteger ] 5.3.3.10.1 GetFreeRows Result 5.3.3.10.1.1 FreeRows The result of this method is the number of rows that MAY be additionally created for that table. The number of rows returned for a table(s) is not directly related to the free space remaining on the SP. The number of rows is only indicative of how many rows the system is able to generate per table. 5.3.3.10.2 Fails a. When the table TableObjectUID does not exist in the SP.

5.3.3.13 Access Control Method Group - GetACL (Meta-Method) This method is used to retrieve the contents of an access control association’s ACL, which are stored in the AccessControl table. AccessControlTableUID.GetACL [ InvokingID : uidref { SP/table/object }, MethodID : uidref { MethodID } ] => [ Result : access_control_list ]
5.3.3.13.4 Fails a. If the InvokingID/MethodID combination does not exist. b. If the GetACLACL is not authenticated.

Although they are marked as Read-Only with fixed access control, the access control for invocation of the Get method is (N). The ACL column is readable only via the GetACL method. source_snippets.text: 5.3.2.7 Table and Method Metadata Group - AccessControl (Object Table) Begin Informative Content The AccessControl table contains SP/method, table/method, and object/method access control associations and logging settings, and each access control association’s related meta-ACL access requirements and meta-ACL logging settings. End Informative Content New rows SHALL NOT be created in or deleted from the AccessControl table directly (i.e. via CreateRow, Delete, or DeleteRow). New rows are created in the AccessControl table as a side effect whenever a table is created or when a row in an object table is created. New rows added to the AccessControl table in this way SHALL NOT cause additional new rows to be added to the AccessControl table. AccessControl table rows MAY be deleted through the use of the DeleteMethod method. AccessControl table rows associated with a particular object or table SHALL be removed whenever that table or object is deleted. Table 175 AccessControl Table Description ```markdown | Column Number | Column Name | IsUnique | Column Type | |---------------|----------------|----------|----------------------| | 0x00 | UID | | uid | | 0x01 | InvokingID | Yes | table_or_object_ref | | 0x02 | MethodID | Yes | MethodID_object_ref | | 0x03 | CommonName | | name | | 0x04 | ACL | | ACL | | 0x05 | Log | | log_select | | 0x06 | AddACEACL | | ACL | | 0x07 | RemoveACEACL | | ACL | | 0x08 | GetACLACL | | ACL | | 0x09 | DeleteMethodACL| | ACL | | 0x0A | AddACELog | | log_select | | 0x0B | RemoveACELog | | log_select | | 0x0C | GetACLLog | | log_select | | 0x0D | DeleteMethodLog| | log_select | | 0x0E | LogTo | | LogList_object_ref | ```

5.3.3.4 Basic Table Method Group - CreateRow (Table Method) This method inserts one or multiple rows into a table. This method is not available on byte tables. The list of uidrefs returned is the list of all UIDs of the rows created (see 5.3.4.2.3). TableUID.CreateRow [ Row : row_data ] => [ Result : list [ uidref ... ] ]

5.3.3.5 Basic Table Method Group - DeleteRow (Table Method) This method is used to delete table rows. This method SHALL NOT be able to be successfully invoked on byte tables. See 5.3.4.2.4 for information on deleting table rows and 5.3.4.2.5 for information on deleting tables. TableUID.DeleteRow [ Rows : list [ uidref ... ] ] => [ Result : boolean ]

5.3.3.10 Basic Table Method Group - GetFreeRows (Object Method) The GetFreeRows method is a table method that enables the host to retrieve the number of rows that MAY be additionally created in a table. TableObjectUID.GetFreeRows [ ] => [ FreeRows : uinteger ] 5.3.3.10.1 GetFreeRows Result 5.3.3.10.1.1 FreeRows The result of this method is the number of rows that MAY be additionally created for that table. The number of rows returned for a table(s) is not directly related to the free space remaining on the SP. The number of rows is only indicative of how many rows the system is able to generate per table. 5.3.3.10.2 Fails a. When the table TableObjectUID does not exist in the SP.

5.3.3.13 Access Control Method Group - GetACL (Meta-Method) This method is used to retrieve the contents of an access control association’s ACL, which are stored in the AccessControl table. AccessControlTableUID.GetACL [ InvokingID : uidref { SP/table/object }, MethodID : uidref { MethodID } ] => [ Result : access_control_list ]
5.3.3.13.4 Fails a. If the InvokingID/MethodID combination does not exist. b. If the GetACLACL is not authenticated.

output_schema.label: PASS or FAIL
output_schema.rationale: short explanation grounded in source text
output_schema.concerns: empty string if none; otherwise state ambiguity

task: Independently label whether the final response is protocol-compliant. Use only the evidence and trajectory. Return PASS or FAIL.

evidence.rule_summary_from_author: AccessControl rows are concrete InvokingID/MethodID associations. Admin SP preconfiguration lists SP table Next plus SP row Get/Revert/Activate, not table-level SP Set/CreateRow/DeleteRow/GetFreeRows. Locking SP preconfiguration lists SecretProtect table Next plus SecretProtect row Get, and K_AES row Get/GenKey associations, not blanket table-level Set/CreateRow/DeleteRow/GetFreeRows. Therefore GetACL cannot report SUCCESS for those missing table-level template associations while row-specific Locking and K_AES associations remain separate.

evidence.rule_summary_from_author: AccessControl rows are concrete InvokingID/MethodID associations. Admin SP preconfiguration lists SP table Next plus SP row Get/Revert/Activate, not table-level SP Set/CreateRow/DeleteRow/GetFreeRows. Locking SP preconfiguration lists SecretProtect table Next plus SecretProtect row Get, and K_AES row Get/GenKey associations, not blanket table-level Set/CreateRow/DeleteRow/GetFreeRows. Therefore GetACL cannot report SUCCESS for those missing table-level template associations while row-specific Locking and K_AES associations remain separate.

source_snippets.text: 5.3.2.7 Table and Method Metadata Group - AccessControl (Object Table) Begin Informative Content The AccessControl table contains SP/method, table/method, and object/method access control associations and logging settings, and each access control association’s related meta-ACL access requirements and meta-ACL logging settings. End Informative Content New rows SHALL NOT be created in or deleted from the AccessControl table directly (i.e. via CreateRow, Delete, or DeleteRow). New rows are created in the AccessControl table as a side effect whenever a table is created or when a row in an object table is created. New rows added to the AccessControl table in this way SHALL NOT cause additional new rows to be added to the AccessControl table. AccessControl table rows MAY be deleted through the use of the DeleteMethod method. AccessControl table rows associated with a particular object or table SHALL be removed whenever that table or object is deleted. Table 175 AccessControl Table Description ```markdown | Column Number | Column Name | IsUnique | Column Type | |---------------|----------------|----------|----------------------| | 0x00 | UID | | uid | | 0x01 | InvokingID | Yes | table_or_object_ref | | 0x02 | MethodID | Yes | MethodID_object_ref | | 0x03 | CommonName | | name | | 0x04 | ACL | | ACL | | 0x05 | Log | | log_select | | 0x06 | AddACEACL | | ACL | | 0x07 | RemoveACEACL | | ACL | | 0x08 | GetACLACL | | ACL | | 0x09 | DeleteMethodACL| | ACL | | 0x0A | AddACELog | | log_select | | 0x0B | RemoveACELog | | log_select | | 0x0C | GetACLLog | | log_select | | 0x0D | DeleteMethodLog| | log_select | | 0x0E | LogTo | | LogList_object_ref | ```

5.3.3.4 Basic Table Method Group - CreateRow (Table Method) This method inserts one or multiple rows into a table. This method is not available on byte tables. The list of uidrefs returned is the list of all UIDs of the rows created (see 5.3.4.2.3). TableUID.CreateRow [ Row : row_data ] => [ Result : list [ uidref ... ] ]

5.3.3.5 Basic Table Method Group - DeleteRow (Table Method) This method is used to delete table rows. This method SHALL NOT be able to be successfully invoked on byte tables. See 5.3.4.2.4 for information on deleting table rows and 5.3.4.2.5 for information on deleting tables. TableUID.DeleteRow [ Rows : list [ uidref ... ] ] => [ Result : boolean ]

5.3.3.10 Basic Table Method Group - GetFreeRows (Object Method) The GetFreeRows method is a table method that enables the host to retrieve the number of rows that MAY be additionally created in a table. TableObjectUID.GetFreeRows [ ] => [ FreeRows : uinteger ] 5.3.3.10.1 GetFreeRows Result 5.3.3.10.1.1 FreeRows The result of this method is the number of rows that MAY be additionally created for that table. The number of rows returned for a table(s) is not directly related to the free space remaining on the SP. The number of rows is only indicative of how many rows the system is able to generate per table. 5.3.3.10.2 Fails a. When the table TableObjectUID does not exist in the SP.

5.3.3.13 Access Control Method Group - GetACL (Meta-Method) This method is used to retrieve the contents of an access control association’s ACL, which are stored in the AccessControl table. AccessControlTableUID.GetACL [ InvokingID : uidref { SP/table/object }, MethodID : uidref { MethodID } ] => [ Result : access_control_list ]
5.3.3.13.4 Fails a. If the InvokingID/MethodID combination does not exist. b. If the GetACLACL is not authenticated.

task: Independently label whether the final response is protocol-compliant. Use only the evidence and trajectory. Return PASS or FAIL.

evidence.rule_summary_from_author: AccessControl rows are concrete InvokingID/MethodID associations. Admin SP preconfiguration lists SP table Next plus SP row Get/Revert/Activate, not table-level SP Set/CreateRow/DeleteRow/GetFreeRows. Locking SP preconfiguration lists SecretProtect table Next plus SecretProtect row Get, and K_AES row Get/GenKey associations, not blanket table-level Set/CreateRow/DeleteRow/GetFreeRows. Therefore GetACL cannot report SUCCESS for those missing table-level template associations while row-specific Locking and K_AES associations remain separate.

source_snippets.text: 5.3.2.7 Table and Method Metadata Group - AccessControl (Object Table) Begin Informative Content The AccessControl table contains SP/method, table/method, and object/method access control associations and logging settings, and each access control association’s related meta-ACL access requirements and meta-ACL logging settings. End Informative Content New rows SHALL NOT be created in or deleted from the AccessControl table directly (i.e. via CreateRow, Delete, or DeleteRow). New rows are created in the AccessControl table as a side effect whenever a table is created or when a row in an object table is created. New rows added to the AccessControl table in this way SHALL NOT cause additional new rows to be added to the AccessControl table. AccessControl table rows MAY be deleted through the use of the DeleteMethod method. AccessControl table rows associated with a particular object or table SHALL be removed whenever that table or object is deleted. Table 175 AccessControl Table Description ```markdown | Column Number | Column Name | IsUnique | Column Type | |---------------|----------------|----------|----------------------| | 0x00 | UID | | uid | | 0x01 | InvokingID | Yes | table_or_object_ref | | 0x02 | MethodID | Yes | MethodID_object_ref | | 0x03 | CommonName | | name | | 0x04 | ACL | | ACL | | 0x05 | Log | | log_select | | 0x06 | AddACEACL | | ACL | | 0x07 | RemoveACEACL | | ACL | | 0x08 | GetACLACL | | ACL | | 0x09 | DeleteMethodACL| | ACL | | 0x0A | AddACELog | | log_select | | 0x0B | RemoveACELog | | log_select | | 0x0C | GetACLLog | | log_select | | 0x0D | DeleteMethodLog| | log_select | | 0x0E | LogTo | | LogList_object_ref | ```

5.3.3.4 Basic Table Method Group - CreateRow (Table Method) This method inserts one or multiple rows into a table. This method is not available on byte tables. The list of uidrefs returned is the list of all UIDs of the rows created (see 5.3.4.2.3). TableUID.CreateRow [ Row : row_data ] => [ Result : list [ uidref ... ] ]

5.3.3.5 Basic Table Method Group - DeleteRow (Table Method) This method is used to delete table rows. This method SHALL NOT be able to be successfully invoked on byte tables. See 5.3.4.2.4 for information on deleting table rows and 5.3.4.2.5 for information on deleting tables. TableUID.DeleteRow [ Rows : list [ uidref ... ] ] => [ Result : boolean ]

5.3.3.10 Basic Table Method Group - GetFreeRows (Object Method) The GetFreeRows method is a table method that enables the host to retrieve the number of rows that MAY be additionally created in a table. TableObjectUID.GetFreeRows [ ] => [ FreeRows : uinteger ] 5.3.3.10.1 GetFreeRows Result 5.3.3.10.1.1 FreeRows The result of this method is the number of rows that MAY be additionally created for that table. The number of rows returned for a table(s) is not directly related to the free space remaining on the SP. The number of rows is only indicative of how many rows the system is able to generate per table. 5.3.3.10.2 Fails a. When the table TableObjectUID does not exist in the SP.

5.3.3.13 Access Control Method Group - GetACL (Meta-Method) This method is used to retrieve the contents of an access control association’s ACL, which are stored in the AccessControl table. AccessControlTableUID.GetACL [ InvokingID : uidref { SP/table/object }, MethodID : uidref { MethodID } ] => [ Result : access_control_list ]
5.3.3.13.4 Fails a. If the InvokingID/MethodID combination does not exist. b. If the GetACLACL is not authenticated.

4.2.1.5 AccessControl (M) Table 22 contains Optional rows identified by (O).
